---
title: "3.7 Class Variables and Methods"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Class Variables and Methods

### `static` belongs to the class, not to one object

The textbook connects this topic back to familiar examples:

::: {.tight-list}
- `Math.random()` is a <span class="term">class method</span>
- `main` is also a static method
- static members are associated with the <span class="mark">class itself</span>
:::

This topic separates shared class behavior from per-object behavior.

## Class Methods

### Static methods are called with the class name

![Calling static and instance methods](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-7-class-variables-and-methods/FlowChartCallingMethods.png){fig-align="center" width="78%"}

```java
ClassName.methodName();
```

::: {.tight-list}
- class methods belong to the class, not to any particular object
- there is only one copy for the whole class
- they can call other static methods directly
- they can access <span class="term">class variables</span>
:::

## Static Context Rules

### A static method cannot directly use instance state

The key AP rule here is:

::: {.tight-list}
- static methods cannot directly access <span class="mark">instance variables</span>
- static methods cannot directly call <span class="mark">instance methods</span>
- they need an object reference passed in or created first
:::

This is why `main` must construct an object before calling instance methods on it.

## Class Variables

### One shared copy exists for the whole class

```java
public class Person
{
    public static int personCounter = 0;
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- class variables are marked with `static`
- every object shares the same class variable
- they are useful for counters, totals, min/max tracking, and similar shared state
- public static variables are accessed with `ClassName.variableName`
:::

## `final` Keyword

### Use `final` for constants that should not change

```java
public static final double PI = 3.14159;
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `final` means the value cannot be modified
- constants are traditionally written in capital letters
- `static final` is a common pattern for shared constants
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish static methods from instance methods
- explain why static methods cannot directly use instance state
- trace shared behavior of a static counter across multiple objects
- identify appropriate uses for `static` and `final`
- <span class="term">3.7.4 Coding Challenge: Static Song and counter</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain what makes a method or variable `static`
- state that static methods cannot directly access instance variables
- explain that class variables are shared across all objects
- identify when the class name should be used to access a member
- explain what `final` means for a variable
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
